name: Water Service Lateral
description: Water Lateral lines are service lines that connect the home or business to the main water line.

name: Water Main
description: Water Mains are pipes that distribute water from a water supply system.

name: Fire Hydrant
description: Fire Hydrants are devices to obtain water from the main supply, consisting of a barrel, connections for hose, and a valve assembly.

name: Water Meter
description: Water Meters are used to measure the volume of water used by residential and commercial buildings.

name: Bridge
description: Bridges and Tunnels within the City of Spokane

name: Traffic Flow
description: Traffic flow counts for arterial streets within the City of Spokane

name: Sewer Force Main
description: Sanitary sewer forced(pressurized) mains

name: Sewer Gravity Main
description: Sanitary sewer gravity mains

name: Stormwater Force Main
description: Shows the generalized location of storm water force (pressurized) mains

name: Stormwater Gravity Main
description: Shows the generalized location of storm water gravity mains

name: Sewer Manhole
description: Sanitary sewer manhole access points

name: Sewer Outfall
description: Show the locations where sanitary sewer has the potential to outfall to surface water

name: Stormwater Inlet
description: Shows the generalized locations of storm water inlets (storm drains)

name: Stormwater Manhole
description: Shows the generalized locations of storm water manhole access points

name: Stormwater Outfall
description: Shows the locations where storm water has the potential to outfall to the surface

name: Final Plat
description: Shows outlines of finalized subdivision plats

name: Landuse
description: Represents Land Use Plan designations as defined in the City of Spokane's Comprehensive Plan. The Land Use Plan designations were adopted in April, 2011 per Ordinance C34693. This data is updated annually to reflect the Comprehensive Plan Ammendment process.

name: Preliminary Plat
description: Shows outlines of preliminary subdivision plats

name: Zoning
description: Polygons representing the zoning designations for the City of Spokane. The data is updated regularly to reflect the current municipal code and associated amendments. See the following link for more information: https://my.spokanecity.org/smc/?Title=17C

name: Bike Plan
description: This dataset provides the Planned Bikeway Network designations as apopted with the Master Bike Plan for the City of Spokane Comprehensive Plan on June 8, 2009 per ordinance C34424.

name: Permit
description: This contains building and engineering permits for the previous 3 calendar years. The data is created nightly from a export of the permit system. Points are geocoded using provided location data such as Parcel ID and addresses.

name: Aquifer
description: This is an outline of the physical extent of the Spokane Valley - Rathdrum Prairie Aquifer. The aquifer outline was described by the USGS in a 1977 Spokane Aquifer study, and was digitized by Spokane County Engineers in 1988. The USGS relied heavily on contour lines that described the valley floor to delineate the Spokane aquifer boundary. The original boundary had the Spokane River as the west edge, but was revised (by Bea Lackaff Spokane County WQMP-GIS) in June, 1994, under the direction of S

name: Environmental Records
description: The Public Works Environmental Programs department has a collection of records ranging from around the years 1985-2012 that consist primarily of soil testing results, remediation efforts, and environmental analysis reports for locations around the City. All of these physical records have been digitized into separate PDFs and are mapped in a GIS layer. The majority of these records are for properties that were once owned, or currently owned, by the City of Spokane, or were sites that had document

name: Erodible Soil
description: Soils with severe ersion hazards extracted from NRCS 1968 Soils survey

name: FEMA Flood Zone
description: This data was adopted by the City of Spokane via Ordinance C34612 amending SMC Title 17E.030 effective 07/06/2010. The Digital Flood Insurance Rate Map (DFIRM) Database depicts flood risk information and supporting data used to develop the risk data. The primary risk classifications used are the 1-percent-annual-chance flood event, the 0.2-percent-annual- chance flood event, and areas of minimal flood risk. The DFIRM Database is derived from Flood Insurance Studies (FISs), previously published F

name: Hazardous Geology
description: This layer is a selection of geologic formations identified by Washington State Department of Natural Resources and adopted into the Spokane County Critical Area Ordinance as having a high susceptibility for landslides. The origin of the data is from USGS at 1:24,000 scale for the urban area of the City of Spokane and Spokane County and 1:100,000 for the rural area. This information is used as a flag only and not a substitute for a field verification by a qualified landslide or erosion specialis

name: Riparian Habitat Area
description: Riparian Habitat Areas as defined in Section 17E.020.050 of the City of Spokane Municipal Code.

name: Shoreline Buffer
description: Shoreline buffers serve several purposes, including filtering pollutants and sediment from runoff, preventing shoreline erosion, preserving fish and wildlife habitat, screening noise, preserving aesthetic values, and helping to achieve no net loss of shoreline ecological functions. Standards for shoreline buffers shall ensure no net loss of shoreline ecological functions and shall preserve the existing character of the shoreline, consistent with the entire SMP.

name: Shoreline Jurisdiction
description: The "Shoreline Jurisdiction" shall refer to the shoreline areas of the Spokane River and Latah Creek within the Spokane City limits where the Shoreline Regulations shall apply, the general boundaries of which are illustrated on the Shoreline Jurisdiction Map, SMC 17E.060.060. In the event that any of the boundaries on the Shoreline Jurisdiction Map conflict with the criteria of WAC 173-22-040 as amended, the criteria shall control. The Shoreline Jurisdiction shall include: 1. All water bodies an

name: Wetland
description: This data set represents the extent, approximate location and type of wetlands and deepwater habitats in the United States and its Territories. These data delineate the areal extent of wetlands and surface waters as defined by Cowardin et al. (1979). The National Wetlands Inventory - Version 2, Surface Waters and Wetlands Inventory was derived by retaining the wetland and deepwater polygons that compose the NWI digital wetlands spatial data layer and reintroducing any linear wetland or surface w

name: Tree Inventory
description: City of Spokane Parks Department tree inventory

name: Building
description: Building footprints digitized from high resolution ortho-photography

name: Park
description: Park boundary polygons

name: Waterbody
description: Polygon features representing waterbodies

name: Curbline
description: The edge lines of paved areas showing the presence or absence of curbs

name: Railroad
description: Railroad Centerlines

name: Sidewalk
description: The sidewalks along the street or in other public areas

name: Street
description: The centerline of a streets traveled way

name: Address
description: Master address point locations database for the City of Spokane

name: Curb Ramp
description: Curb ramp location points

name: Annexation History
description: Polygons showing when different land areas were annexed into the City of Spokane

name: CD Neighborhood
description: Community Development Neigborhood boundary polygons delineate eligablity for community block grants

name: City Council
description: City of Spokane council district boundaries created with respect to 2020 Census population data (per Ordinance number C34800).  Shapefile update provide by Spokane City Council was derived using Dave's Redistricting tools (https://davesredistricting.org/maps#home).

name: County Boundary
description: Spokane county boundary

name: Fire District
description: This version of Fire Districts contains the unserved and contracted areas as polygons.

name: Municipal
description: Municipal boundaries in Spokane County WA

name: Neighborhood
description: Neighborhood council boundaries for the City of Spokane

name: School District
description: School districts in Spokane County WA

name: Voting Precinct
description: A precinct is generally the smallest level of geography established for purposes of conducting elections. Each precinct has a specific location where its residents go to vote.

name: Business Improvement District
description: Business Improvement Districts (BIDs) are adopted by City Ordinance and guided by sections of the Spokane Municipal Code. These districts generally include the assessment of dues or fees paid by property owners to the BID fund, which are then used for the provision of certain programs that foster greater success for local businesses and services as well as (sometimes) the planning, construction, and maintenance of certain public improvements and infrastructure.

name: Foreign Trade Zone
description: Foreign trade zones (FTZ) are areas in and around points of entry to the Country where commercial merchandise, both foreign and domestic, receives special consideration under traffic and tax burdens. These areas are designated by the federal government and are thus not subject to local approval or changes. The Spokane FTZ is classified as FTZ-224.

name: Historic District
description: National Historic Districts are groups of associated properties within a specific boundary that are listed on the National Register of Historic Places. National historic districts are formed pursuant to the National Historic Preservation Act. Properties within national historic districts are protected from demolition via provisions in Spokane Municipal Code Section 17D.100.230.

name: Local Historic District
description: Local Historic Districts are an overlay zone composed of associated properties within a specific boundary that are listed on the Spokane Register of Historic Places. Local historic district overlay zones are formed pursuant to provisions in Spokane Municipal Code Chapter 17D.100. Properties within local historic districts are protected via provisions in the Spokane Municipal Code.

name: Low Income Housing Tax Credit
description: The Low-Income Housing Tax Credit program was established by the 1986 Tax Reform Act and is administered by the federal department of Housing and Urban Development. The program allocates tax credits to development to encourage the construction and rehabilitation of affordable multifamily housing. This feature class indicates which Census tracts in the City of Spokane are eligible for the program. Note, these areas are set by agencies outside the City of Spokane.

name: Multi Family Tax Exemption
description: These areas are those in which qualified multi-family developments can apply for a multi-family tax exemption (MFTE), which exempts certain property taxes for a range of years, dependent on the location and whether they include affordable units or not. Those areas within the City’s Targeted Investment Areas are eligible for 8, 12, or 20 year exemptions (depending on requirements) and outside areas are eligible for either 12 or 20 years. These areas of eligibility were set by Resolution RES 2022-

name: New Market Tax Credit
description: Qualified US Census tracts as defined by the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund (CDFI) of the US Treasury for the New Markets Tax Credit Program (NMTC). The Target Investment Areas represents those Census tracts identified as “Distressed” under the NMTC. Note, these areas are set by agencies outside the City of Spokane.

name: Public Development Authorities
description: Business Improvement Districts (BIDs) are adopted by City Ordinance and guided by the Spokane Municipal Code. These districts generally include the assessment of dues or fees paid by property owners to the BID fund, which are then used for the provision of certain programs that foster greater success for local businesses and services as well as (sometimes) the planning, construction, and maintenance of certain public improvements and infrastructure.

name: Qualified Opportunity Zone
description: A Qualified Opportunity Zone (QOZ) is a designated area or community where new investments, under certain conditions, may be eligible for preferential tax treatment. Areas are nominated for designation by the State and certified by the US Treasure. This feature class identifies which portions of the City of Spokane are within a designated QOZ. Note, these areas are set by agencies outside the City of Spokane.

name: Spokane Target Investment Area
description: The Target Investment Areas are based on qualified US Census tracts as defined by the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund (CDFI) of the US Treasury for the New Markets Tax Credit program (NMTC). The Target Investment Areas represents those Census tracts identified as “Distressed” under the NMTC. Note, these areas are set by agencies outside the City of Spokane.

name: Tax Increment Financing
description: Tax Increment Financing (TIF) districts are distinct areas for which a portion of property taxes is allocated for the purposes of constructing certain public infrastructure projects within the district(s). These are generally adopted under a number of State law provisions and are usually adopted either by the City, the County, or both.

name: Historic Property
description: Historic Properties are single properties that are individually listed on the Spokane Register of Historic Places or the National Register of Historic Places. Properties listed on the historic register are protected via provisions in the Spokane Municipal Code Chapter 17D.100.

name: SPD Ticket and Citation
description: Table containing records of citations, tickets, and infractions issued by City of Spokane Police Department (SPD) officers. This data represents officer-initiated field activity where a citation was issued. Location information is available by the Neighborhood or Precinct, but exact locations have been redacted to protect privacy. This data dates back to 1/1/2019.

name: SPD Offenses
description: Table containing criminal offense records generated by the City of Spokane Police Department (SPD). This table includes records where an officer or report taker determined a Call For Service would be documented in a police report. This includes emergency and non-emergency Calls for Service (CFS), self-initiated online reporting, officer-initiated incidents, among other forms of reporting. The data contains case number, incident type, crime code, date and time, and a generalized description of th

name: SPD Collisions
description: Table containing emergency and non-emergency Calls for Service from the City of Spokane Police Department (SPD) dispatched to traffic collisions/accidents. Includes officer-initiated incidents. Attributes include call type, call source, time received, and a generalized description of the call request. Location information is provided in the form of a street address or intersection, or Latitude and Longitude, or both. This data dates back to 1/1/2017.

name: SPD Calls For Service
description: Table containing Neighborhood or Police Block locations of emergency and non-emergency Calls For Service including call type, time received, and a generalized description of the call request. Exact locations have been redacted to protect privacy. This data dates back to 1/1/2019.

name: Census Tracts 2010
description: Contains the data compiled from the questions asked of all people and about every housing unit. Population items include sex, age, race, Hispanic or Latino origin, household relationship, household type, household size, family type, family size, and group quarters. Housing items include occupancy status, vacancy status, and tenure (whether a housing unit is owner-occupied or renter-occupied).For a full description of data fields see: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2010/doc/sf1.pdf

name: Census Block Groups 2010
description: Contains the data compiled from the questions asked of all people and about every housing unit. Population items include sex, age, race, Hispanic or Latino origin, household relationship, household type, household size, family type, family size, and group quarters. Housing items include occupancy status, vacancy status, and tenure (whether a housing unit is owner-occupied or renter-occupied).For a full description of data fields see: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2010/doc/sf1.pdf

name: Census Block 2010
description: Contains the data compiled from the questions asked of all people and about every housing unit. Population items include sex, age, race, Hispanic or Latino origin, household relationship, household type, household size, family type, family size, and group quarters. Housing items include occupancy status, vacancy status, and tenure (whether a housing unit is owner-occupied or renter-occupied).For a full description of data fields see: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2010/doc/sf1.pdf

name: Contours
description: 2' elevation contours covering the Spokane, WA metro area. Contours meet ASPRS Class I accuracy standards. Developed from 2007 LIDAR and orthophotography.
